016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.conversion;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonParser;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonProcessingException;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonToken;
021import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.DeserializationContext;
022import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonDeserializer;
023import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.InvalidFormatException;
024import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
025import org.bitcoinj.base.AddressParser;
026import org.bitcoinj.base.Network;
027import org.bitcoinj.base.exceptions.AddressFormatException;
028
029import java.io.IOException;
030
031/**
032 * Deserialize bitcoinj (family) addresses
033 */
034public class AddressDeserializer extends JsonDeserializer<Address> {
035    private final AddressParser addressParser;
036
037    /**
038     * Construct an address deserializer that will deserialize addresses for any of the default supported networks.
039     * See {@link Network} to understand what the supported networks are.
040     */
041    public AddressDeserializer() {
042        this(AddressParser.getDefault());
043    }
044
045    /**
046     * Construct an address deserializer that validates addresses for the specified {@link Network}.
047     * When deserializing addresses, addresses that are not from the specified network will cause a
048     * {@link InvalidFormatException} to be thrown during deserialization.
049     *
050     * @param network Network id to specify the only network we will deserialize addresses for.
051     */
052    public AddressDeserializer(Network network) {
053        this(AddressParser.getDefault(network));
054    }
055
056    /**
057     * Construct an address deserializer with a custom {@link AddressParser}
058     * @param addressParser parser to convert a string to an address
059     */
060    public AddressDeserializer(AddressParser addressParser) {
061        this.addressParser = addressParser;
062    }
063
064    @Override
065    public Address deserialize(JsonParser p, DeserializationContext ctxt) throws IOException, JsonProcessingException {
066        JsonToken token = p.getCurrentToken();
067        switch (token) {
068            case VALUE_STRING:
069                try {
070                    return addressParser.parseAddress(p.getValueAsString());
071                } catch (AddressFormatException e) {
072                    throw new InvalidFormatException(p, "Invalid Address: " + p.getValueAsString(), p.getValueAsString(), Address.class);
073                }
074            default:
075                return (Address) ctxt.handleUnexpectedToken(Address.class, p);
076        }
077    }
078}
